nativeRange.habitat: Creeks and small rivers along the Thai-Myanmar border, growing in iron-rich clay soil with calcium carbonate; submerged during the wet season and emergent during the dry season.

decorPreferences: open foreground with strong light penetration
moderate flow for dense carpet formation

summary: Downoi is a compact foreground plant from Thailand and Myanmar, recognised by its tight, curly star-shaped leaves arranged in rosettes. Generally considered intermediate in difficulty, it does best under strong light, supplemental CO2, and a nutrient-rich substrate.
careNotes: Pogostemon helferi is a distinctive carpeting plant from the Thai-Myanmar border, prized for its tight curly-edged leaves arranged in rosettes that resemble small green stars. Generally placed in the foreground, where it tends to stay compact (around 5-8 cm) under strong light and grows taller (up to 15 cm) under dimmer conditions. Has been reported to be sensitive to nutrient deficiencies — iron and magnesium shortfalls commonly present as chlorosis (yellowing) or stunted, pale new growth, while excessive macronutrients can suppress growth or trigger melting. A nutrient-rich aquasoil with regular comprehensive fertilization (macros plus iron-rich trace elements) is generally advised. Stable water parameters matter: sudden swings can cause rapid melt.
breedingNotes: Propagates vegetatively in the aquarium, primarily through lateral shoots that emerge from the base of mature plants. To replant, the parent clump can be lifted and divided into 4-5 smaller blocks spaced roughly 3-5 cm apart; senescent leaves and excess roots should be trimmed before replanting. Head cuttings are possible but generally less successful. Flowering (small lavender blooms, 15-30 mm) typically only occurs on emersed-grown specimens.

primary.name: Tropica
primary.notes: Primary source: native range, taxonomy, propagation, height. Tropica rates difficulty as among the easiest foreground plants and CO2 as optional; this disagrees with Buce Plant and Aquariumbreeder.

additional.name: Buce Plant
additional.notes: Secondary source for pH (6-7.5), lighting (medium-high), CO2 (recommended), care difficulty (medium). Buce Plant collections root had no direct downoi listing; product page used instead.

additional.name: Aquariumbreeder
additional.notes: Third source for cross-verification: temperature 22-26°C, pH 6-8, gH 6-18, lighting 30-50 PAR, CO2 required, substrate aquasoil, propagation via lateral shoots. Used to average the temp/pH/hardness ranges with Tropica.
